text: Git branching is a powerful feature of version control that helps teams work efficiently without interfering with each other’s changes. Developers commonly use feature branches to build new functionality separately from the main project. Each feature branch can be worked on independently, and once the work is complete, it is merged back into the main branch. To ensure code quality, many teams use pull requests, which allow others to review the code before merging. This workflow keeps the project stable and well-organized. By following a clear version control workflow, teams can collaborate smoothly and manage changes effectively.
